title: Developer Experience & Advanced APIs
description: Phase 10 focuses on developer experience improvements and advanced APIs. It introduces transaction batching for bulk operations, graph change events for real-time sync, incremental TF-IDF indexing, and intelligent query cost estimation.
goals: 2-3x speedup for bulk operations via transaction batching
Real-time change notifications via EventEmitter-based events
5-10x faster ranked search updates via incremental TF-IDF
Intelligent automatic algorithm selection for optimal search performance

sprints.title: Transaction Batching API
sprints.summary: Create fluent transaction API for batching multiple graph operations in a single lock/save cycle. Provides 2-3x speedup for bulk operations.

sprints.title: Graph Change Events
sprints.summary: Create type-safe EventEmitter wrapper for graph change notifications. Enables real-time sync, audit logging, and cache invalidation.
keyDeliverables: Event types for entity/relation CRUD operations
Lazy initialization for zero overhead when unused

sprints.title: Incremental TF-IDF Index
sprints.summary: Add incremental update methods to TFIDFIndexManager for O(log n) single-entity updates instead of O(n) full rebuilds. Hooks into graph events for automatic updates.
keyDeliverables: Dirty term tracking with lazy IDF recalculation

sprints.title: Query Cost Estimation
sprints.summary: Create QueryCostEstimator for analyzing queries and recommending optimal search algorithms. Enables intelligent automatic algorithm selection.
keyDeliverables: Algorithm recommendation based on query and graph
search_auto MCP tool for automatic selection

executionOrder.reason: Transaction batching is foundational - provides the atomic operation pattern used by event emission
Graph events depend on transaction batching for testing combined event emission
Incremental TF-IDF depends on graph events for automatic index updates
Query cost estimation is independent but best done last to incorporate learnings

risksMitigations.risk: Transaction batching adds complexity to GraphStorage
risksMitigations.mitigation: BatchTransaction class is separate from existing TransactionManager, minimizing coupling
Lazy initialization ensures zero overhead when no listeners registered

risksMitigations.risk: Incremental IDF recalc may have edge cases
risksMitigations.mitigation: Fallback to full rebuild if dirty term count exceeds threshold
Include confidence score and explanation in estimates

successMetrics: 5-10x faster ranked search updates for single entity changes
postPhaseActions: Benchmark all new features against Phase 9 baselines
Update CLAUDE.md with new features documentation
Consider Phase 11: Query optimization based on cost estimates
